editableNewDialogue.text: I’m stuck on a spreadsheet cleanup step—what’s a quick way to remove extra spaces so my lookups don’t fail?
Use TRIM to remove leading/trailing (and repeated) spaces: =TRIM(A2). If there may be non‑breaking spaces from web exports, wrap it: =TRIM(SUBSTITUTE(A2,CHAR(160)," ")).
And if I want to make sure two columns match exactly after cleaning—what’s a simple check?
Create a helper column with an exact comparison. In Excel: =EXACT(cleaned_col1, cleaned_col2) returns TRUE/FALSE. If you just need a mismatch flag, you can use: =IF(cleaned1<>cleaned2,"Mismatch","").
Switching gears—what’s the difference between COUNT(*) and COUNT(column) in SQL?
COUNT(*) counts rows (including rows where every column is NULL). COUNT(column) counts only rows where that specific column is NOT NULL.
Random: I saw a headline about a telescope sending back new images—how do those get processed?

Typically: calibration (remove sensor noise/dark frames), correction (flat-fielding, distortion), alignment/stacking (combine exposures), and then color mapping/contrast stretching for scientific and public-release versions. Different instruments (e.g., infrared vs visible) have different pipelines, but the stages are similar.

For the Ros-Lehtinen internship bullets, can you keep the wording tight and accurate? It was the Miami district office, so most of what I did was casework intake and community outreach rather than anything legislative.

Absolutely—here’s a tighter version that keeps it grounded in district-office work:

**Congressional Intern** — Office of Congresswoman Ileana Ros-Lehtinen (District Office, Miami, FL)  
- Supported constituent services by logging and triaging casework requests, following up on open items, and escalating time-sensitive issues to permanent staff.  
- Volunteered with the re-election team on outreach activities and event support to strengthen constituent engagement.  
- Helped bridge communication between parts of the Venezuelan exile community and the Congresswoman’s office by coordinating touchpoints, sharing updates, and ensuring concerns were routed to the appropriate staff.  
- Built working relationships with contacts across Capitol Hill and partner offices through briefings and interoffice coordination to support constituent and community-facing needs.
Quick correction — I’m actually based in Washington, DC and I’m open to any Deloitte office. Rosslyn isn’t a preference anymore.
Got it—thanks for clarifying. I’ll remove the Rosslyn-specific line and keep it general (open to any Deloitte office), and if you want, we can note Washington, DC as your base.
